items.caption:
Comment “STEAM” 😍🔗👇👇👇Easily one of my favorite cleaning tools! 

It has replaced FIVE cleaning products in my home:
- floor cleaner
- shower and tub cleaner
- magic erasers 
- grout cleaner / brush 
- glass & mirror cleaner

It’s such a powerhouse 💪😍 and one of my TOP recommended cleaning tools.

What’s your favorite cleaning tool? 🤘🩷

#cleaning #cleanwithme #steamcleaned #homemakingtips

items.caption:
Comment “TUBS” and I’ll send you the 🔗👇👇👇 

What a lot of organizers won’t tell you is that you don’t have to make your space fit the containers - you should make the containers fit YOUR space. If you even need them 🤷‍♀️ 

In this case - I definitely did 😆🫣 but my old setup wasn’t working (it wasn’t the giant stack shown in this video, but it wasn’t working, so I ditched the system I was using for the giant stack 😁). 

And that’s the cycle…

Try one system —> 
realize it doesn’t fit your needs so you ditch it —> 
end up with a giant stack or pile —> 
figure out something that actually DOES work for your space and needs. 🫰 

I knew I wanted four containers, preferably different colors (for easy sorting and organizing for both me and the kids) and I needed them much wider than the 2” vertical containers I was using. 

Those would be able to hold one, maybe two books - nothing more 😕

This works so much better for us, and while it might not be as beige or clear as someone else’s homeschool setup - it works 👏. And that’s the real goal in home organization, am I right? 

Skip what everyone else thinks would or should work. Measure your workbooks (or items!) and find containers that fit around the measurements you need. 

#homeorganizer #organized #homeschool #homeschoolroom #organizedhouse

items.caption:
Just a crafty girl in love with her laser cutting machine 🙋‍♀️😆😍 

I looooove crafting but I don’t always have the patience for some of the crafting hobbies out there like needlepoint, paper crafts, etc. Laser cutting is so quick and easy, though, (after a quick dial in on settings!) and I’ve been so impressed by the performance of my Thunder lasers. 

I love creating own own home decor like this for each holiday or birthday! 

I started out with the BOLT and moved to the @thunderlaserusa Nova Plus 35 60W (the laser in this video 👋) and both blew my mind 🥰🙌. 

Should I make this a file for tiered tray decor? 🤔 

[thunder laser partner] #laserengraved #lasercuttingmachine #laserprojects #4thofjuly #crafts

musicInfo.song_name: AMERICAN GIRLS x MIDNIGHT CITY (ALTÉGO MIX)

items.ownerFullName: Corinne Burghardt 🏡 ✨ Slay At Home Mother

items.caption:
In honor of America’s 250th birthday this year! Let’s make a door sign… ❤️🤍💙

I absolutely LOVE making #doorsigns with my laser cutter. It’s such a fun way to use up the laser bed space and make something I can use for years and years to come! I imagine door signs would be a great money maker as well 💰 😁

I always go with a color that contrasts our front door (black), but you could totally paint this or make it any patriotic color you like 😍. I think a red circle against a white door would look amazing 🤩 . 

Materials used:
- spray paint
- 3mm Baltic birch wood
- starbond medium glue 
- wood stain 
- wired ribbon 2”
- @thunderlaserusa Nova Plus 35 60W #lasercuttingmachine 💪⚡️

#crafts #laserengraved #lasercutter [Thunder laser partner 👋]

items.ownerFullName: Corinne Burghardt 🏡 ✨ Slay At Home Mother

items.caption:
Homeschool curriculum choices for the upcoming school year 🙏🫶 

Please note that I will not be debating anyone on the different curriculums, this will be our second year so I’m far from an expert on that. 😁

Here’s what we chose:
- @thegoodandthebeautiful math and language arts/handwriting
- @masterbooks soil, sea, and sky elementary science pack 
- @notgrasshistory Our 50 States pack 
- TGTB U.S. Constitution & Government bundle 
- @notconsumed daily apologetics Faithipedias (old & new testaments) 
- Google Sheets planner 

I say all this knowing life happens and we’ll have sick days, play dates, a co-op 🙏 , Awana, field trips - not everything will go according to plan 😆🫣 but us #homeschoolmoms can try, right? 😁 

And by mapping things out, I can get a better sense of what’s to come 🥰

I think Icame into the first year not nearly as organized and prepped as I found out I needed to be so I *could* pivot - but now I’ve got my feet on more solid ground 💪

items.ownerFullName: Corinne Burghardt 🏡 ✨ Slay At Home Mother
items.transcript: I get asked this question so I'm sharing my curriculum choices for the upcoming school year starting with the Good and the Beautiful for my first grader. This is mostly for comfort because this is what we chose for our first year diving in and the kids really seem to take to it. I do enjoy the geographical and cultural tie-ins. We may branch out later from it though, we'll see. We're also using this curriculum for my fifth grader at grade level although I'm using the fourth grade version of handwriting workbook because that's where he's at. I love that the math book comes with an answer key so I can quickly check his answers and we can get going to extracurriculars, meeting up with friends, etc. For science this year I chose this set from Masterbooks. This set is more for my fifth grader but there's also a book for my first grader to help her really dive into the subject as well. For social studies I got this book on Amazon I figure we can use in the car to learn a bit about each day in the US but we're also doing a deep dive into the 50 states with the set from Knotgrass. I fell in love with this because of all the recipes and hands-on activities included in the lessons. My kids really respond to that. For our foreign language we're really just doing an introductory short lesson and learning different words in Spanish. This book from Amazon comes with a free app as well which we have enjoyed so far. Back to social studies we're also going to be diving into the US Constitution from the Good and the Beautiful. Faith-wise I'm going to start off with these faithopedias from Knot Consumed for Old Testament and New Testament and then we also have some conversation starters. I think this is from Knot Consumed as well, really great. And finally I have a new Google Sheets document I'm using to coordinate my lessons this year just to help us keep track because that really helps me.
